name: Poulet a L'intrigue (Chicken)
recipeIngredient: 3 lb Boneless chicken breast, skin on
recipeInstructions:
MARINADE:
1. In a large bowl, mix 1 cup peanut oil, 1 cup hickory barbecue sauce, 1 cup water, 6 tablespoons white wine Worcestershire, 4 tablespoons all-purpose seasoning, 1/2 cup lime juice, 1 tablespoon minced fresh garlic, and 2 tablespoons chopped onion.
STUFFING:
2. In a large skillet, melt 4 ounces salt-free butter over medium heat. Add 10 ounces sausage and cook until browned.
3. Add 1/2 cup chopped onion, 1/2 cup chopped celery, 1 red bell pepper (diced), 1 shredded carrot, 1 garlic clove (chopped), 1 tablespoon all-purpose seasoning, and 1 cup chicken stock to the skillet. Stir well.
4. Add 2 cups chopped mushrooms, 1/2 teaspoon dried parsley, 1/2 teaspoon chopped green onions, and 1/2 loaf French bread (diced) to the skillet. Turn off heat.
SAUCE:
5. In a saucepan, melt 6 tablespoons butter over medium heat. Stir in 3 tablespoons flour until smooth.
6. Gradually whisk in 2-1/4 cups milk, stirring constantly until the mixture thickens and coats the back of a spoon.
7. Add 1 cup Chardonnay, 1 teaspoon all-purpose seasoning, 2 cups chicken stock, 1/4 cup whipping cream, 1 tablespoon parsley, 1 tablespoon green onions, 1/4 pound Andouille sausage (diced), and 1/4 pound pecans (chopped) to the saucepan. Stir until combined.
8. Simmer for 5 minutes over low heat.
Chicken:
9. Marinate chicken breast in the marinade mixture for 24 hours.
10. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Bake chicken breasts for 15 minutes, then remove and lightly smoke with hickory chips for an additional 10 minutes.
11. Serve chicken on a platter, topped with sauce.
Note: All-purpose seasoning is prepared by combining equal parts white pepper, black pepper, red pepper flakes, onion powder, garlic powder, thyme, chili powder, dill, paprika, mustard, basil, tarragon, and oregano in a 10% salt mixture.
